item.title: Ultraprocessed foods may be linked with dementia risk, study finds
item.description: A new study suggests a link between ultra-processed foods and an increased risk of dementia in older adults. Researchers found that a group of people who reported eating diets high in ultraprocessed foods had a 58% higher risk of developing dementia later in life and a 46% increased risk of developing cognitive impairment. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: Pence's former chief of staff warns Iran war threatens farmer vote for GOP
item.description: A new op-ed in the Washington Post suggests the war in Iran and President Trump's trade policy risk Republicans losing the support of a key coalition: farmers. Republican strategist Marc Short, who wrote the piece, and Democratic strategist Mo Elleithee join with analysis.

item.title: House votes to rein in Trump on Iran as war loses GOP support
item.description: In May, the Senate advanced a similar measure to force the president to end the prolonged conflict with Iran.

item.title: Major takeaways from primaries in California, Iowa
item.description: Primary election votes are still being counted in California, but Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will head to a runoff to defend her seat. In Iowa, a Trump-backed candidate for governor lost his race and Democrats are hoping the results will help them flip the seat in November. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: What to know as House passes war powers resolution to limit Trump's authority on Iran
item.description: The House passed a resolution Wednesday to block President Trump from ordering more strikes on Iran. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has more details.

item.title: U.S.-Iran tensions escalate with new strikes despite ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. and Iran had one of the most intense back-and-forths since their ceasefire was announced nearly two months ago. Iran struck Kuwait International Airport overnight, killing one person and leaving dozens more wounded. The regime said these strikes were in retaliation for U.S. strikes on Iranian military targets, while the U.S. said it acted in self-defense. CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

item.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman meets with lawmakers in wake of Trump AI order
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman met with Republican and Democratic lawmakers on Wednesday to oppose proposals that would require developers to obtain government approval before releasing new artificial intelligence models. Jordan Rae Kelly, former director for cyber incident response on the National Security Council, joins with analysis.

item.title: Iran calls deadly attack on Kuwait retaliation for latest U.S. strikes
item.description: After Trump insisted talks with Iran were "going on continuously," Iran fired a deadly missile and drone salvo at Kuwait, claiming retaliation for new U.S. strikes.

item.title: Rep. Joyce Beatty lauds passage of Iran war powers resolution, judge's order on Kennedy Center name
item.description: A federal judge last week ordered the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center and also barred the performing arts center from closing its doors for proposed renovations. Democratic Rep. Joyce Beatty of Ohio, who brought the lawsuit and also serves on the board of trustees, joins to discuss that and Wednesday's House passage of an Iran war powers resolution.

item.title: Trump confirms he called Netanyahu "f***ing crazy" during phone call
item.description: Earlier this week, Axios was the first to report details of the phone conversation between President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in which Mr. Trump called his Netanyahu "f***ing crazy." Axios White House reporter Marc Caputo broke the story and joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: What to make of Tuesday's primaries in California and Iowa
item.description: The dust has settled in Iowa's primaries and is beginning to settle in California. CBS News' Julie Watts, Lana Zak and Anthony Salvanto have the latest on the most important and undercovered races in both states.

item.title: Trump says Iran talks ongoing despite attack on Kuwait
item.description: Iran launched deadly strikes on Kuwait on Wednesday, targeting U.S. bases as part of what it described as self-defense strikes. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has the latest on President Trump's reaction.

item.title: A look at San Antonio and New York City ahead of Spurs-Knicks NBA Finals
item.description: Game 1 of the NBA Finals will tip off Wednesday in San Antonio between the Spurs and the New York Knicks. CBS News' Jason Allen and Jared Ochacher have more.

item.title: Microsoft VP explains new AI assistant Scout
item.description: Microsoft announced a slew of new AI products during its annual Microsoft Build developers conference in San Francisco. Scott Hanselman, a Microsoft vice president, speaks with CBS News about the product.

item.title: Meta unveils new AI business agent for customer service
item.description: Businesses can now expand their AI customer service with Meta's help. Senior technology reporter Abrar Al-Heeti joins CBS News to talk about that and some of the other big tech stories of the day.

item.title: House passes Iran war powers resolution for first time
item.description: The House passed a measure Wednesday that would force President Trump to seek congressional approval for the Iran war. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Sam Forstag projected to win Democratic primary in Montana's 1st District
item.description: Democrats are hoping to pick up the open seat. GOP Rep. Ryan Zinke is retiring at the end of his term.

item.title: Victim of "horrifying" Chelsea murder identified after 26 years
item.description: "Chelsea Jane Doe" has been identified as Tiffany Bradley of Pennsylvania.

item.title: NASA gives up on lost Mars orbiter
item.description: NASA officials said the $582 million MAVEN orbiter could not be recovered after a problem on the far side of Mars late last year, and that its extraordinarily successful mission was at an end.

item.title: Possible flesh-eating screwworm case in Texas, USDA says
item.description: A possible case of the flesh-eating New World screwworm is being investigated in Texas, the USDA reported Wednesday.

item.title: Trump says negotiations with Iran are still happening
item.description: President Trump answered reporters' questions on Wednesday from the Oval Office. He covered the "anti-weaponization" fund, Iran, negotiating for the first time with Hezbollah and more topics. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: Senate advances reconciliation bill, dropping ballroom funds ahead of final push
item.description: Senate Republicans moved forward with a package to fund the Department of Homeland Security's immigration agencies Wednesday.

item.title: What we know about the suspect who held 10 people hostage at Southern California bank
item.description: All hostages are safe and a suspect is dead after an hourslong standoff at a bank in California, police said on Wednesday. CBS News correspondent Carter Evans has the details.

item.title: American Airlines suspends 6 routes because of high jet fuel costs
item.description: American Airlines said it is temporarily cutting six routes amid rising jet fuel costs, including several flights to Los Angeles.

item.title: California governor's race narrows as Hilton, Becerra take early lead
item.description: Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer have emerged as the leading contenders to advance to November's general election, according to early counts.

item.title: Ford recalls nearly 420,000 vehicles over faulty seat belts
item.description: Safety watchdog said seat belts in certain Ford Expedition and Lincoln vehicles may inadvertently lock, preventing them from functioning properly.

item.title: What travelers should know about Europe's new Entry/Exit System
item.description: Travel experts say to be prepared for potential disruptions as countries implement the new Entry/Exit System now in place across the EU and other countries.

item.title: Hostage situation at bank building in California ends after suspect shot dead
item.description: The suspect was pronounced dead at the scene early Wednesday morning, the Bakersfield Police Department said.

item.title: 8 bagged bodies found with note in narco-trafficking hotspot
item.description: The discovery comes in the midst of a U.S.-backed military crackdown on the organized crime gangs in Ecuador.

item.title: Teen released by ICE wants to stay in U.S.: "Doing the right things"
item.description: Ricardo Hernandez-Navarrete graduated from high school after being released by ICE, but he and his mother still face the possibility of deportation.
